pattern: Testing whether a string is built from the first letters of a word list is a single synchronized pass: the lengths must line up one-to-one, and each position's letter must match the corresponding word's opening character, in order.
hints: The candidate string can only be a match if it has exactly one character for every word in the list — check that length relationship before comparing anything else.
Walk the word list and the string together, position by position, comparing each word's first character to the character at that same position in the string.
The moment any position disagrees, or the lengths never matched to begin with, the answer is false; only agreeing all the way through makes it true.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, repeated first letters collapse the wrong way
statement example, six words matching a six-letter acronym
single one-letter word exactly matching a one-letter string
maximum word count, each word contributing to a maximum-length acronym
acronym is a strict prefix of what the words actually spell, length mismatch must fail it
same first letters present but in the wrong order
duplicate first letters across different words, order still must line up

stress.description: random word lists paired with either their true acronym or an unrelated random string
